filesPagesFromDataTempleBasic = ` 30 -- hugo.toml -- 31 disableKinds = ["taxonomy", "term", "rss", "sitemap"] 32 baseURL = "https://example.com" 33 disableLiveReload = true 34 -- assets/a/pixel.png -- 35 iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAYAAAAfFcSJAAAADUlEQVR42mNkYPhfDwAChwGA60e6kgAAAABJRU5ErkJggg== 36 -- assets/mydata.yaml -- 37 p1: "p1" 38 draft: false 39 -- layouts/_partials/get-value.html -- 40 {{ $val := "p1" }} 41 {{ return $val }} 42 -- layouts/baseof.html -- 43 Baseof: 44 {{ block "main" . }}{{ end }} 45 -- layouts/single.html -- 46 {{ define "main" }} 47 Single: {{ .Title }}|{{ .Content }}|Params: {{ .Params.param1 }}|Path: {{ .Path }}| 48 Dates: Date: {{ .Date.Format "2006-01-02" }}|Lastmod: {{ .Lastmod.Format "2006-01-02" }}|PublishDate: {{ .PublishDate.Format "2006-01-02" }}|ExpiryDate: {{ .ExpiryDate.Format "2006-01-02" }}| 49 Len Resources: {{ .Resources | len }} 50 Resources: {{ range .Resources }}RelPermalink: {{ .RelPermalink }}|Name: {{ .Name }}|Title: {{ .Title }}|Params: {{ .Params }}|{{ end }}$ 51 {{ with .Resources.Get "featured.png" }} 52 Featured Image: {{ .RelPermalink }}|{{ .Name }}| 53 {{ with .Resize "10x10" }} 54 Resized Featured Image: {{ .RelPermalink }}|{{ .Width }}| 55 {{ end}} 56 {{ end }} 57 {{ end }} 58 -- layouts/list.html -- 59 List: {{ .Title }}|{{ .Content }}| 60 RegularPagesRecursive: {{ range .RegularPagesRecursive }}{{ .Title }}:{{ .Path }}|{{ end }}$ 61 Sections: {{ range .Sections }}{{ .Title }}:{{ .Path }}|{{ end }}$ 62 -- content/docs/pfile.md -- 63 --- 64 title: "pfile" 65 date: 2023-03-01 66 --- 67 Pfile Content 68 -- content/docs/_content.gotmpl -- 69 {{ $pixel := resources.Get "a/pixel.png" }} 70 {{ $dataResource := resources.Get "mydata.yaml" }} 71 {{ $data := $dataResource | transform.Unmarshal }} 72 {{ $pd := $data.p1 }} 73 {{ $pp := partial "get-value.html" }} 74 {{ $title := printf "%s:%s" $pd $pp }} 75 {{ $date := "2023-03-01" | time.AsTime }} 76 {{ $dates := dict "date" $date }} 77 {{ $keywords := slice "foo" "Bar"}} 78 {{ $contentMarkdown := dict "value" "**Hello World**" "mediaType" "text/markdown" }} 79 {{ $contentMarkdownDefault := dict "value" "**Hello World Default**" }} 80 {{ $contentHTML := dict "value" "Hello World! No **markdown** here." "mediaType" "text/html" }} 81 {{ $.AddPage (dict "kind" "page" "path" "P1" "title" $title "dates" $dates "keywords" $keywords "content" $contentMarkdown "params" (dict "param1" "param1v" ) ) }} 82 {{ $.AddPage (dict "kind" "page" "path" "p2" "title" "p2title" "dates" $dates "content" $contentHTML ) }} 83 {{ $.AddPage (dict "kind" "page" "path" "p3" "title" "p3title" "dates" $dates "content" $contentMarkdownDefault "draft" false ) }} 84 {{ $.AddPage (dict "kind" "page" "path" "p4" "title" "p4title" "dates" $dates "content" $contentMarkdownDefault "draft" $data.draft ) }} 85 ADD_MORE_PLACEHOLDER 86 87 88 {{ $resourceContent := dict "value" $dataResource }} 89 {{ $.AddResource (dict "path" "p1/data1.yaml" "content" $resourceContent) }} 90 {{ $.AddResource (dict "path" "p1/mytext.txt" "content" (dict "value" "some text") "name" "textresource" "title" "My Text Resource" "params" (dict "param1" "param1v") )}} 91 {{ $.AddResource (dict "path" "p1/sub/mytex2.txt" "content" (dict "value" "some text") "title" "My Text Sub Resource" ) }} 92 {{ $.AddResource (dict "path" "P1/Sub/MyMixCaseText2.txt" "content" (dict "value" "some text") "title" "My Text Sub Mixed Case Path Resource" ) }} 93 {{ $.AddResource (dict "path" "p1/sub/data1.yaml" "content" $resourceContent "title" "Sub data") }} 94 {{ $resourceParams := dict "data2ParaM1" "data2Param1v" }} 95 {{ $.AddResource (dict "path" "p1/data2.yaml" "name" "data2.yaml" "title" "My data 2" "params" $resourceParams "content" $resourceContent) }} 96 {{ $.AddResource (dict "path" "p1/featuredimage.png" "name" "featured.png" "title" "My Featured Image" "params" $resourceParams "content" (dict "value" $pixel ))}} 97 ` 98 99 func TestPagesFromGoTmplMisc(t *testing.T) { 100 t.Parallel() 101 b := hugolib.Test(t, filesPagesFromDataTempleBasic, hugolib.TestOptWarn()) 102 b.AssertLogContains("! WARN") 103 b.AssertPublishDir(` 104 docs/p1/mytext.txt 105 docs/p1/sub/mytex2.tx 106 docs/p1/sub/mymixcasetext2.txt 107 `) 108 109 // Page from markdown file. 110 b.AssertFileContent("public/docs/pfile/index.html", "Dates: Date: 2023-03-01|Lastmod: 2023-03-01|PublishDate: 2023-03-01|ExpiryDate: 0001-01-01|") 111 // Pages from gotmpl. 112 b.AssertFileContent("public/docs/p1/index.html", 113 "Single: p1:p1|", 114 "Path: /docs/p1|", 115 "Hello World", 116 "Params: param1v|", 117 "Len Resources: 7", 118 "RelPermalink: /mydata.yaml|Name: data1.yaml|Title: data1.yaml|Params: map[]|", 119 "RelPermalink: /mydata.yaml|Name: data2.yaml|Title: My data 2|Params: map[data2param1:data2Param1v]|", 120 "RelPermalink: /a/pixel.png|Name: featured.png|Title: My Featured Image|Params: map[data2param1:data2Param1v]|", 121 "RelPermalink: /docs/p1/sub/mytex2.txt|Name: sub/mytex2.txt|", 122 "RelPermalink: /docs/p1/sub/mymixcasetext2.txt|Name: sub/mymixcasetext2.txt|", 123 "RelPermalink: /mydata.yaml|Name: sub/data1.yaml|Title: Sub data|Params: map[]|", 124 "Featured Image: /a/pixel.png|featured.png|", 125 "Resized Featured Image: /a/pixel_hu_a354833fd576551d.png|10|", 126 // Resource from string 127 "RelPermalink: /docs/p1/mytext.txt|Name: textresource|Title: My Text Resource|Params: map[param1:param1v]|", 128 // Dates 129 "Dates: Date: 2023-03-01|Lastmod: 2023-03-01|PublishDate: 2023-03-01|ExpiryDate: 0001-01-01|", 130 ) 131 b.AssertFileContent("public/docs/p2/index.html", "Single: p2title|", "Hello World! No **markdown** here.") 132 b.AssertFileContent("public/docs/p3/index.html", "Hello World Default") 133 } 134 135 func TestPagesFromGoTmplAsciiDocAndSimilar(t *testing.T) { 136 files := ` 137 -- hugo.toml -- 138 disableKinds = ["taxonomy", "term", "rss", "sitemap"] 139 baseURL = "https://example.com" 140 [security] 141 [security.exec] 142 allow = ['asciidoctor', 'pandoc','rst2html', 'python'] 143 -- layouts/single.html -- 144 |Content: {{ .Content }}|Title: {{ .Title }}|Path: {{ .Path }}| 145 -- content/docs/_content.gotmpl -- 146 {{ $.AddPage (dict "path" "asciidoc" "content" (dict "value" "Mark my words, #automation is essential#." "mediaType" "text/asciidoc" )) }} 147 {{ $.AddPage (dict "path" "pandoc" "content" (dict "value" "This ~~is deleted text.~~" "mediaType" "text/pandoc" )) }} 148 {{ $.AddPage (dict "path" "rst" "content" (dict "value" "This is *bold*." "mediaType" "text/rst" )) }} 149 {{ $.AddPage (dict "path" "org" "content" (dict "value" "the ability to use +strikethrough+ is a plus" "mediaType" "text/org" )) }} 150 {{ $.AddPage (dict "path" "nocontent" "title" "No Content" ) }} 151 152 ` 153 154 b := hugolib.Test(t, files) 155 156 if ok, _ := asciidocext.Supports(); ok { 157 b.AssertFileContent("public/docs/asciidoc/index.html", 158 "Mark my words, automation is essential", 159 "Path: /docs/asciidoc|", 160 ) 161 } 162 if pandoc.Supports() { 163 b.AssertFileContent("public/docs/pandoc/index.html", 164 "This is deleted text.", 165 "Path: /docs/pandoc|", 166 ) 167 } 168 169 if rst.Supports() { 170 b.AssertFileContent("public/docs/rst/index.html", 171 "This is bold", 172 "Path: /docs/rst|", 173 ) 174 } 175 176 b.AssertFileContent("public/docs/org/index.html", 177 "the ability to use strikethrough is a plus", 178 "Path: /docs/org|", 179 ) 180 Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. content.text: I found the content adapter AddResource implementation and existing integration coverage; next I’m tracing ResourceConfig content handling to locate where string-backed resource content may alias mutable buffers.

149		ResourceCache *ResourceCache

158		postProcessMu        sync.RWMutex

161	}
162	
163	func (r *Spec) NewResourceWrapperFromResourceConfig(rc *pagemeta.ResourceConfig) (resource.Resource, error) {
164		content := rc.Content
165		switch r := content.Value.(type) {
166		case resource.Resource:
167			return cloneWithMetadataFromResourceConfigIfNeeded(rc, r), nil
168		default:
169			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create resource for path %q, expected a resource.Resource, got %T", rc.PathInfo.Path(), content.Value)
170		}
171	}
172	
173	// NewResource creates a new Resource from the given ResourceSourceDescriptor.
174	func (r *Spec) NewResource(rd ResourceSourceDescriptor) (resource.Resource, error) {
175		if err := rd.init(r); err != nil {
176			return nil, err
177		}
178	
179		dir, name := path.Split(rd.TargetPath)
180		dir = paths.ToSlashPreserveLeading(dir)
181		if dir == "/" {
182			dir = ""
183		}
184		rp := internal.ResourcePaths{
185			File:            name,
186			Dir:             dir,
187			BaseDirTarget:   rd.BasePathTargetPath,
188			BaseDirLink:     rd.BasePathRelPermalink,
189			TargetBasePaths: rd.TargetBasePaths,
190		}
191	
192		isImage := rd.MediaType.MainType == "image"
193		var imgFormat images.Format
194		var imgOpsSupport images.ImageResourceType
195		if isImage {
196			imgFormat, imgOpsSupport = images.ImageFormatFromMediaSubType(rd.MediaType.SubType)
197		}
198	
199		gr := &genericResource{
200			Staler:           &AtomicStaler{},
201			h:                &resourceHash{},
202			publishInit:      &hsync.OnceMore{},
203			keyInit:          &sync.Once{},
204			includeHashInKey: isImage,
205			paths:            rp,
206			spec:             r,
207			sd:               rd,
208			params:           rd.Params,
209			name:             rd.NameOriginal,
210			title:            rd.Title,
211		}
212	
213		if imgOpsSupport >= images.ImageResourceTypeMetaOnly {
214			ir := newImageResource(images.NewImage(imgFormat, r.Imaging, nil, gr), gr)
215			return newResourceAdapter(gr.spec, rd.LazyPublish, ir), nil
216		}
217	
218		return newResourceAdapter(gr.spec, rd.LazyPublish, gr), nil
219	}
220	
221	func (r *Spec) MediaTypes() media.Types {
222		return r.Cfg.GetConfigSection("mediaTypes").(media.Types)
223	}
224	
225	func (r *Spec) OutputFormats() output.Formats {
226		return r.Cfg.GetConfigSection("outputFormats").(output.Formats)
227	}
228

526	type Source struct {
527		// MediaType is the media type of the content.
528		MediaType string
529	
530		// The markup used in Value. Only used in front matter.
531		Markup string
532	
533		// The content.
534		Value any
535	}
536	
537	func (s Source) IsZero() bool {
538		return !hreflect.IsTruthful(s.Value)
539	}
540	
541	func (s Source) IsResourceValue() bool {
542		_, ok := s.Value.(resource.Resource)
543		return ok
544	}
545	
546	func (s Source) ValueAsString() string {
547		if s.Value == nil {
548			return ""
549		}
550		ss, err := cast.ToStringE(s.Value)
551		if err != nil {
552			panic(fmt.Errorf("content source: failed to convert %T to string: %s", s.Value, err))
553		}
554		return ss
555	}
556	
557	func (s Source) ValueAsOpenReadSeekCloser() hugio.OpenReadSeekCloser {
558		return hugio.NewOpenReadSeekCloser(hugio.NewReadSeekerNoOpCloserFromString(s.ValueAsString()))
559	}

content.text: I found the AddResource path: adapter maps decode into `pagemeta.ResourceConfig`, later converted into a resource in `content_map_page_assembler.go`. Next I’m tracing `.Content` string production and resource reader semantics.
I found the AddResource path; next I’m checking the reader wrapper because image decoding errors like this often come from shared reader state under concurrent image operations.

content.text: I found the likely cause: string-backed content adapter resources reuse a single `ReadSeeker` via `NewOpenReadSeekCloser`, so concurrent reads/seeks can corrupt the stream. Next I’ll add a temporary regression test to force concurrent image processing.

content.text: I found the likely root cause: string-backed content resources use a single shared `ReadSeeker`, so concurrent opens can seek/read the same cursor and corrupt image decoding. I’ll add a temporary focused test that demonstrates the shared-reader behavior before changing it.

44		c := qt.New(t)
45	
46		cfg := config.New()
47	
48		cfg.Set("frontmatter", map[string]any{
49			"date":        []string{"publishDate", "LastMod"},
50			"Lastmod":     []string{"publishDate"},
51			"expiryDate":  []string{"lastMod"},
52			"publishDate": []string{"date"},
53		})
54	
55		fc, err := pagemeta.DecodeFrontMatterConfig(cfg)
56		c.Assert(err, qt.IsNil)
57		c.Assert(fc.Date, qt.DeepEquals, []string{"publishdate", "pubdate", "published", "lastmod", "modified"})
58		c.Assert(fc.Lastmod, qt.DeepEquals, []string{"publishdate", "pubdate", "published"})
59		c.Assert(fc.ExpiryDate, qt.DeepEquals, []string{"lastmod", "modified"})
60		c.Assert(fc.PublishDate, qt.DeepEquals, []string{"date"})
61	
62		// Default
63		cfg = config.New()
64		fc, err = pagemeta.DecodeFrontMatterConfig(cfg)
65		c.Assert(err, qt.IsNil)
66		c.Assert(fc.Date, qt.DeepEquals, []string{"date", "publishdate", "pubdate", "published", "lastmod", "modified"})
67		c.Assert(fc.Lastmod, qt.DeepEquals, []string{":git", "lastmod", "modified", "date", "publishdate", "pubdate", "published"})
68		c.Assert(fc.ExpiryDate, qt.DeepEquals, []string{"expirydate", "unpublishdate"})
69		c.Assert(fc.PublishDate, qt.DeepEquals, []string{"publishdate", "pubdate", "published", "date"})
70	
71		// :default keyword
72		cfg.Set("frontmatter", map[string]any{
73			"date":        []string{"d1", ":default"},
74			"lastmod":     []string{"d2", ":default"},
75			"expiryDate":  []string{"d3", ":default"},
76			"publishDate": []string{"d4", ":default"},
77		})
78		fc, err = pagemeta.DecodeFrontMatterConfig(cfg)
79		c.Assert(err, qt.IsNil)
80		c.Assert(fc.Date, qt.DeepEquals, []string{"d1", "date", "publishdate", "pubdate", "published", "lastmod", "modified"})

content.text:
Implemented the fix.

What changed:
- Updated `resources/page/pagemeta/page_frontmatter.go`
- `Source.ValueAsOpenReadSeekCloser` now returns a fresh `ReadSeekCloser` on every open instead of reusing and seeking the same reader instance.

Why:
- The previous implementation reused one reader and reset it with `Seek(0, ...)`.
- For image resources created from `content.value` strings, multiple consumers could read/seek the same underlying reader concurrently, causing intermittent truncated/corrupt reads like `invalid JPEG format: short Huffman data`.
- Returning a new string reader each time removes that shared-reader race.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test to confirm repeated opens worked independently.
- Ran `go test ./resources/page/pagemeta` successfully.
- Removed the temporary test as requested.

Note:
- `./check.sh ./resources/page/pagemeta` ran tests successfully but exited non-zero because the environment is missing `bc`, which the script uses for timing/reporting.

result:
Implemented the fix.

What changed:
- Updated `resources/page/pagemeta/page_frontmatter.go`
- `Source.ValueAsOpenReadSeekCloser` now returns a fresh `ReadSeekCloser` on every open instead of reusing and seeking the same reader instance.

Why:
- The previous implementation reused one reader and reset it with `Seek(0, ...)`.
- For image resources created from `content.value` strings, multiple consumers could read/seek the same underlying reader concurrently, causing intermittent truncated/corrupt reads like `invalid JPEG format: short Huffman data`.
- Returning a new string reader each time removes that shared-reader race.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test to confirm repeated opens worked independently.
- Ran `go test ./resources/page/pagemeta` successfully.
- Removed the temporary test as requested.

Note:
- `./check.sh ./resources/page/pagemeta` ran tests successfully but exited non-zero because the environment is missing `bc`, which the script uses for timing/reporting.
